dom.title: World News: Top & Breaking World News Today | AP News
dom.bodyTextSample: Enable accessibility Menu WORLD U.S. POLITICS SPORTS ENTERTAINMENT BUSINESS SCIENCE MORE Sign in Show Search WORLD NEWS IRAN WAR RUSSIA-UKRAINE WAR ESPAÑOL CHINA ASIA PACIFIC LATIN AMERICA EUROPE Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state TV reports A ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz while using a route not approved by Iran. That’s according to a report Wednesday by state television in Tehran. 42 As the Pentagon stays quiet, AP reconstructs a US strike that killed over 100 Iranian children 249 Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries plunge Russia into a summer fuel crisis 9

anchors.text: Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state TV reports
Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries plunge Russia into a summer fuel crisis
As the Pentagon stays quiet, AP reconstructs a US strike that killed over 100 Iranian children
The Morning Wire Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.
The Afternoon Wire Get caught up on what you may have missed throughout the day.
Los Angeles medical examiner says former child actor Daveigh Chase died of AIDS
Harvard professor with polarizing alien theories is picked to lead new White House UFO council
Dead baby is found in a portable toilet at a music festival in Michigan
The Morning Wire Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.
Test Your News I.Q. — take today’s quiz
Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, rejecting Trump’s proposed limits
The divided Supreme Court's birthright citizenship decision exposes sharp rifts among justices
Supreme Court will consider whether laws known as assault weapons bans violate the Second Amendment
Ground Game Exclusive insights and key stories from the world of politics.
LeBron James not returning to Lakers, plans to play 24th season elsewhere
Mexico ends a 40-year knockout drought, beats Ecuador 2-0 to reach the round of 16
Mexico fans use a homophobic chant during the World Cup match against Ecuador
AP Top 25 Poll Alerts Get email alerts for every college football Top 25 Poll release.
The Sports Wire Your home base for in-depth reporting from the world of sports.
OpenAI film 'Artificial,' dropped by Amazon, finds a new home with Neon
Japan’s Eiko Kadono, author of ‘Kiki’s Delivery Service,’ still believes in the magic of books at 91
Movie Review: 'Minions & Monsters' is a very yellow mash note to Hollywood
AP Entertainment Wire Get AP's first personalized newsletter delivering you entertainment news twice
Trump filing shows he took in about $1.2 billion from crypto businesses last year
Egg producers will pay $3.3M and donate 53 million eggs to settle price fixing claims
Ford recalling more than 741,00 vehicles because of park system issue
The largest digital camera ever built begins decade-long survey of the universe
Viral phenomenon in Argentina has young people identifying themselves as animals
Raccoon goes on drunken rampage in Virginia liquor store and passes out on bathroom floor
Runaway kangaroo on the loose named Sheila shuts down Alabama interstate
Lettuce introduce you to the live frog found in this grocery store salad bag
Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla living in captivity, celebrates her 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
Nipper, stay! The future of a beloved dog statue on a New York warehouse is up in the air
How to stay cool in a heat wave even without air conditioning
Scratching that bug bite might feel good at first but science explains why it's a bad idea
Questions about resume gaps are expected. Here's how job seekers can address them
A glowing red room in southern Lebanon shows life after the fighting
At the Kaaba during Hajj, a photographer captures sense of unity and devotion
A photo captures a wall of wild horses surging through western Germany
The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.
